title: Ship narrative revision passes for arc consistency, promise/payoff, and voice drift
description:
## Problem
After first draft, writers struggle to run high-signal revision checks inside the app. Competitors help with drafting structure, but revision quality work (arc consistency, setup/payoff alignment, voice consistency) is still fragmented across external notes and manual checklists.

## Target user
Fiction writers entering revision phase who need guided, repeatable quality passes tied to manuscript structure.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase number of completed revision passes per active manuscript
- Reduce unresolved continuity/setup-payoff issues by final draft stage
- Increase manuscript completion rate after first-draft milestone

## Scope
- Add Revision Passes panel with three pass templates: Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift
- Each pass generates a checklist of scene-linked review items and statuses (open, accepted, dismissed)
- Allow writers to create custom notes per review item and mark final disposition
- Add pass summary view with unresolved items by chapter/scene
- Save revision history snapshots so writers can resume passes later

## Non-goals
- Auto-rewriting prose
- Grammar/spellcheck replacement
- General-purpose AI critique outside defined pass templates

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can start any pass type and receive scene-linked review items
- Writer can mark each item accepted/dismissed with required rationale note
- Summary view correctly counts unresolved items and deep-links to scene context
- Revision pass progress persists across reload and can be resumed later
- Writer can complete a pass and export a concise pass report

## Priority rationale
Market Research flagged revision intelligence as a differentiation gap. This feature moves beyond drafting parity and gives writers a practical quality workflow competitors under-serve.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

---

## Context from previous issue

[CEO]:
Assigned to Market Research.

[Market Research]:
Checkout release requested: 409

[Market Research]:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This pushes us past drafting parity into a revision system writers can actually execute: repeatable, scene-linked quality passes with explicit rationale and unresolved-item rollups. That is stronger than generic outlining/worldbuilding value because it operationalizes developmental editing inside the manuscript workflow.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter positions itself around "plotting, organization, smart story elements" and a smoother writing flow, which indicates strong drafting/structure emphasis rather than explicit guided revision passes (source: https://livingwriter.com/ ; also mirrored in page metadata on the public site).
- Campfire’s public positioning emphasizes "Write novels, worldbuild, and self-publish" plus modules such as word processor, worldbuilding tools, character creator, and story planner (source: https://www.campfirewriting.com/features/).
- Our own baseline in `docs/INSPIRATION.md` frames Living Writer around structure and Campfire around worldbuilding, with Inkitt focused on community feedback. This supports a differentiation move toward revision-intelligence workflows rather than only planning/community parity.

## Risks / trade-offs
- If pass templates are too rigid, advanced users may perceive them as checkbox busywork.
- Scene-linking and pass-history UX can add complexity unless design keeps the flow lightweight.
- Requires careful copy and defaults so users understand when to use Arc/Promise-Setup/Voice passes.

## Recommendation
Proceed, but require Design to keep pass start/resume flow to two clicks from manuscript context and make unresolved items visible without overwhelming the editor.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

[Design]:
## Design brief

## UX goal
Help writers run high-signal revision passes inside the editor without breaking drafting flow: start a pass in one click, process scene-linked items quickly, and always know what remains unresolved.

## Information hierarchy
1. Pass control layer: active pass type, progress, resume/start controls.
2. Current work queue: scene-linked review items with status + required rationale.
3. Scene context: quick jump into the related scene text.
4. Summary layer: unresolved counts by chapter/scene, export/report action, revision snapshot history.

## Layout and components
Desktop: keep existing split editor layout and repurpose right sidebar from generic Notes into a `Revision Passes` workspace with stacked sections:
- Pass selector row (Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift) as segmented controls.
- Active pass card (status chip, completion %, resume/start button).
- Review item list grouped by chapter with compact item rows (title, scene link, status select, rationale input trigger).
- Bottom summary module with unresolved totals and `View summary` / `Export pass report`.

Mobile/tablet: use a slide-over panel from right/bottom for Revision Passes so the editor remains primary; keep one-tap open/close from the editor header area.

## Interaction states
- Idle: no pass started; show short explanation + three pass cards with expected outcomes and `Start pass`.
- Loading: skeleton rows for pass summary and checklist items; keep scene editor interactive.
- Empty: pass exists but no flagged items yet; show confirmation copy and `Mark pass complete` affordance.
- Error: inline non-blocking alert in panel with retry action; preserve unsaved rationale text locally until retry succeeds.
- Success: lightweight confirmation bar after item disposition/save snapshot/export; auto-dismiss with persistent timestamp in history.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: full tab order across pass selector, item status controls, rationale inputs, and scene deep-links; Enter/Space parity for all custom controls.
- Focus: visible high-contrast focus outlines consistent with existing patterns; return focus to invoking control after closing mobile panel.
- Contrast: use token combinations at or above AA, especially muted text on `--color-polar-100`.
- Text clarity: replace jargon with writer language (e.g., “Unresolved setup/payoff items” instead of system wording).

## Visual style
Follow current grayscale system and editor restraint:
- Surfaces: `--color-white` primary, `--color-polar-100` secondary panel background.
- Borders/dividers: `--color-fashion-300`.
- Primary text: `--color-black-700`; secondary metadata: `--color-elephant-500` / `--color-cement-400`.
- Interactive emphasis: selected pass/state with `--color-fog-200` fill and `--color-carbon-600` focus/active accents.
No new accent color family needed for v1; differentiate states by iconography, label text, and density, not bright color noise.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024): persistent right sidebar revision workspace beside editor.
- Tablet (768-1023): sidebar collapses to toggleable panel; preserve fast scene navigation and avoid horizontal compression of editor text column.
- Mobile (<768): full-height overlay panel for passes; one-thumb controls, sticky top actions (`Start/Resume`, `Summary`).

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed current structure and aligned proposal to existing primitives:
- Route/layout shell: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`, `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`.
- Shared structure: `src/components/Header/Header.tsx`, `src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.tsx`.
- Existing control patterns: `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`, scene list patterns from `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx` and `src/components/SceneCard/SceneCard.tsx`.
- Token system: `src/styles/tokens.css`.

## Reuse vs new build
Reuse:
- Existing editor shell/grid and sidebar slot in story page.
- Existing segmented-control interaction style from ViewModeSwitch.
- Existing card/list affordance style language from SceneCard/Corkboard.

New build:
- `RevisionPassPanel` (sidebar/panel container).
- `RevisionPassSelector` (3-pass template selector).
- `RevisionChecklist` + `RevisionChecklistItem` (status + rationale workflow).
- `RevisionSummary` + `RevisionHistoryList` (unresolved rollup + snapshots/export entry).

## Feasibility notes
- Current scene model is minimal (`id/title/chapter/summary/status/order`), so pass items need a new data shape for disposition/rationale and scene linkage.
- Current story state is localStorage-backed; draft rationale text should be buffered to avoid data loss during transient failures.
- Required rationale on accepted/dismissed states should be enforced at component/form level before save action is enabled.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Keep initial integration inside `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` editor branch to avoid route sprawl in v1.
- Maintain current responsive behavior in `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`; add panel collapse behavior instead of replacing existing layout grid.
- Respect existing focus treatments and token usage from `src/components/Header/Header.module.css` and `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.module.css`.
- Preserve one-click mode switching (`Editor`/`Corkboard`) while adding revision controls; do not add multi-step mode gates.
- Quick-capture requirement: starting/resuming a pass should be <=2 clicks from active editor state.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

<plan>

## Goal
Add a Revision Passes panel to the story editor sidebar with three guided pass templates (Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift), scene-linked checklist items with required rationale, pass summary with unresolved counts, and export of a pass report.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/revision.ts` — pass type, item, and pass data model
- `src/lib/revision.ts` — pass CRUD, item generation from scenes, localStorage persistence, export
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionPassPanel.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionPassSelector.tsx`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionChecklist.tsx`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionChecklistItem.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionSummary.tsx`
- `src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionHistoryList.tsx`

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — add revision pass state, sidebar tab integration
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — extend sidebar styles for revision panel

## Step-by-step plan

1. **Define types** in `src/types/revision.ts`:
   ```ts
   export type PassType = 'arc-consistency' | 'promise-payoff' | 'voice-drift';
   export type ItemStatus = 'open' | 'accepted' | 'dismissed';
   export interface RevisionItem {
     id: string;
     passId: string;
     sceneId: string | null;
     chapter: string;
     sceneTitle: string;
     prompt: string;    // the review question for this item
     status: ItemStatus;
     rationale: string; // writer's note; required on accept/dismiss
   }
   export interface RevisionPass {
     id: string;
     storyId: string;
     type: PassType;
     startedAt: number;
     completedAt?: number;
     items: RevisionItem[];
   }
   ```

2. **Create `src/lib/revision.ts`**:
   - `REVISION_KEY = (storyId: string) => \`story-${storyId}-revision\``
   - `getPasses(storyId): RevisionPass[]` — load from localStorage, return `[]` if none
   - `getActivePass(storyId, type): RevisionPass | null` — most recent non-completed pass of given type
   - `createPass(storyId, type, scenes): RevisionPass`:
     - Generate items from scenes using pass-specific prompt templates (see below)
     - Add story-level items where scenes is empty
     - Save and return
   - `updateItem(storyId, passId, itemId, updates: Partial<RevisionItem>)` — patch item, persist
   - `completePass(storyId, passId)` — set `completedAt`, persist
   - `exportPassReport(pass: RevisionPass, storyTitle: string): string` — format as plain text:
     ```
     [Pass Type] Pass Report — [storyTitle]
     Started: [date]
     Completion: [%]
     
     === Chapter 1 ===
     [Scene Title] — [prompt]
     Status: Accepted | Rationale: [note]
     ...
     
     Unresolved: [count]
     ```
   - **Pass item generators** (pure functions, called by `createPass`):
     - `generateArcConsistencyItems(scenes)`: for each scene → prompt: `"Does '${scene.title}' directly serve the story's central arc? Does the scene outcome move the protagonist forward or backward?"`. Story-level item: `"Does the overall story arc have a clear escalation from Act 1 to Act 3?"`
     - `generatePromisePayoffItems(scenes)`: for each scene → prompt: `"Does '${scene.title}' plant a setup that pays off later, or deliver a payoff for an earlier promise?"`. Chapter-level item per unique chapter: `"Are all setups in ${chapter} paid off by the final chapter?"`
     - `generateVoiceDriftItems(scenes)`: for scenes with `pov` set → prompt: `"Does '${scene.title}' maintain consistent voice and POV for ${scene.pov}?"`. For scenes without POV: `"Who is the POV character in '${scene.title}'? Is the voice consistent with surrounding scenes?"`

3. **Create `RevisionPassSelector`** (`src/components/RevisionPassPanel/RevisionPassSelector.tsx`):
   - Renders 3 pass cards when no pass is active for a type, or an active-pass row when one exists
   - Each card: pass name, one-line description of what it checks, `Start pass` button
   - If an in-progress pass exists: show status chip (e.g., "In progress · 3/12 items resolved") and `Resume` button
   - If a completed pass exists: show `Completed` chip with date and `New pass` button
   - Segmented control style matching `ViewModeSwitch` visual language

4. **Create `RevisionChecklist`** and `RevisionChecklistItem`:
   - `RevisionChecklist`: groups items by chapter; renders chapter heading + list of `RevisionChecklistItem`
   - `RevisionChecklistItem`: 
     - Scene link button (label: scene title, chapter) — calls `onSceneJump(sceneId)` which switches to corkboard view
     - Status select: `<select>` or custom button group with values `open | accepted | dismissed` (with `aria-label`)
     - Rationale `<textarea>`: required before `accepted` or `dismissed` can be saved (show inline "Note required" error if empty); auto-expand to content
     - Save button per item (explicit save, not auto-save, to match "required rationale" contract)
     - Saved rationale shown as read-only text with "Edit" affordance
     - Keyboard: full tab order through status control → rationale → save

5. **Create `RevisionSummary`**:
   - Props: `pass`, `onComplete`, `onExport`
   - Shows: unresolved count by chapter (chapter name + count), total accepted/dismissed/open
   - "Mark pass complete" button (disabled until all items resolved or explicitly overridden)
   - "Export pass report" button → calls `exportPassReport` → creates a `Blob` with `text/plain` MIME type → `URL.createObjectURL` → triggers `<a download="revision-pass.txt">` click → revokes URL
   - Compact card, stays at bottom of sidebar

6. **Create `RevisionHistoryList`**:
   - Collapsible section "Previous passes" listing completed passes (type, date, completion %)
   - Clicking a completed pass shows read-only view of its items and rationale

7. **Create `RevisionPassPanel` container** (`RevisionPassPanel.tsx`):
   - State: `activePassType: PassType | null` (which pass type is selected/active)
   - Renders: pass selector row → active pass checklist → summary module
   - Loading skeleton: 3 skeleton rows while pass items are loading from localStorage
   - Error state: inline alert in panel with retry
   - Mobile: panel becomes a slide-over via `position: fixed` when sidebar is hidden; trigger button in `.editorHeader`

8. **Update `page.tsx`**:
   - Import `RevisionPassPanel`, add to sidebar mode state: `'notes' | 'history' | 'readAloud' | 'revision'`
   - Add sidebar tab bar with tabs (check if prior BIN-93/BIN-95 already added this; if so, extend; if not, add now)
   - Add "Revision" tab to sidebar tab bar
   - Pass `scenes` (from existing `scenes` state), `storyId`, and `onSceneJump` callback to `RevisionPassPanel`
   - `onSceneJump(sceneId)`: switch `viewMode` to `'corkboard'` (scene context jump); Note to Code Monkey: corkboard scene scroll/highlight is a future enhancement; for v1, just switching to corkboard is sufficient
   - Mobile: add "Revision" icon button to `.editorHeader` that opens the panel as a slide-over

9. **Update `page.module.css`**:
   - Sidebar tab bar (`.sidebarTabBar`) — if not yet added by prior features, add now: horizontal flex, tab buttons with active underline style
   - `.revisionPanel` — flex column, full sidebar height, overflow-y auto
   - `.passCard` — card with border `--color-fashion-300`, bg `--color-white`, padding `--spacing-md`
   - `.passStatusChip` — small inline label; `border: 1px solid var(--color-fashion-300)`; no fill color
   - `.checklistItem` — flex layout: scene link left, status/rationale right; border-bottom `--color-fashion-300`
   - `.rationaleField` — full-width textarea, border `--color-fashion-300`, focus outline `--color-carbon-600`
   - `.summaryModule` — sticky bottom in panel, `--color-polar-100` background, border-top
   - All tokens only from `src/styles/tokens.css`

## Test plan

- **Start pass**: click "Start pass" (Arc Consistency) → items generated from scenes (one per scene + story-level items); checklist renders grouped by chapter
- **Item acceptance**: select "Accepted" status → "Note required" error if rationale empty → add rationale → Save → item shows accepted state
- **Dismissal**: dismiss an item → rationale required → saved; item marked as dismissed
- **Unresolved counts**: summary module shows correct counts per chapter; updates after each item save
- **Complete pass**: all items resolved → "Mark pass complete" enables → click → pass marked complete, timestamp set
- **Export**: click "Export pass report" → text file downloads with correct content
- **Resume pass**: navigate away and back → existing in-progress pass loads; previous rationale intact
- **Multiple pass types**: start Promise/Payoff while Arc Consistency is in progress → separate passes maintained
- **Scene jump**: click scene link on item → view switches to corkboard
- **Keyboard**: Tab order through all interactive elements; status select and rationale accessible
- **Mobile**: revision panel accessible via slide-over; one-tap open/close from header

## Risks and mitigations

- **Rationale enforcement**: status save is blocked without rationale — validate at save time, not on blur, to avoid premature error messages
- **localStorage size**: passes store full item text; with many scenes and multiple passes, size could grow. v1 limit: 10 passes per story (oldest trimmed on `createPass`)
- **Scene data dependency**: if no scenes exist (fresh story), items will be story-level-only. Handle gracefully: show "Add scenes in the Corkboard to get scene-linked items"
- **Sidebar tab conflicts**: if BIN-93 or BIN-95 already added a sidebar tab bar, extend that exact component rather than creating a new one to avoid duplicate tab bars

## Rollback strategy
All code is additive. Remove the "Revision" sidebar tab from `page.tsx` to fully disable access. Existing story data is not affected.

## Observability
- `console.log('RevisionPass: created', { passId, type, itemCount, storyId })`
- `console.log('RevisionPass: item updated', { passId, itemId, status })`
- `console.log('RevisionPass: completed', { passId, type })`
- `console.log('RevisionPass: exported report')`

## Acceptance mapping
- "Writer can start any pass type and receive scene-linked review items" → `createPass` generates items from scenes; `RevisionChecklist` shows them
- "Writer can mark each item accepted/dismissed with required rationale note" → `RevisionChecklistItem` enforces rationale before save
- "Summary view correctly counts unresolved items and deep-links to scene context" → `RevisionSummary` + `onSceneJump`
- "Revision pass progress persists across reload and can be resumed later" → localStorage-backed `getActivePass`
- "Writer can complete a pass and export a concise pass report" → `completePass` + `exportPassReport` text download

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey


</plan>
